usm07_dial_0372: The Russian military organization is highly centralized. If we cut off the head of the snake, it will completely disrupt their attack, and they’ll start running around like headless chickens.
usm07_dial_0373: Option two: we push toward the dam to stop this bomb. Those Delta Force guys are true believers, willing to die for the mission, but I’d like to think they’ll hear us out before pulling the pin.
usm07_dial_0374: Eliminating the risk of a nuclear mushroom popping up in our backyard would give us more time to figure out how to reach the enemy HQ, but it'd also certainly give the Russians more time to prepare.
usm07_dial_0375: Whatever you do, do it fast, Commander! We're on the clock, literally!
usm07_dial_0580: We’ve reached the other bank. I’d like to think that was the hardest part, but I doubt it.
usm07_dial_0586: Under normal circumstances, I’d tell you to secure the high ground before entering that narrow valley. But right now, we don’t have that luxury. We need to move, and fast.
usm07_dial_0581: We’re closing in on their HQ. Keep pushing, we’re almost there!
usm07_dial_0376: We’re coming into range of their HQ. Look at that: supplies, artillery, surface-to-air missiles, all packed in tight, like a powder keg waiting to blow.
usm07_dial_0582: Light ’em up, Commander! Leave nothing standing!
usm07_dial_0378: That was a Fourth of July show they’ll never forget!
usm07_dial_0583:
We did it Commander! Enemy troops are starting to retreat!
Green light team, this is Lieutenant Colonel Williams; abort the mission, the city is ours, I repeat abort the mission!
usm07_dial_0460: Understood, let's cancel the arming procedure and stand down the device.
usm07_dial_0584: After this, Miller can piss off and let us be!
usm07_dial_0585:
You clearly don’t know the man. He’ll never forgive you for standing up to him and he'll make you pay.
But you did the right thing and I'm proud of you.
usm07_dial_0380: Green Light Team here, we have the dam in sight, it's heavily guarded.
usm07_dial_0377: Uh-oh... looks like the horsemen of the apocalypse just showed up.
usm07_dial_0390: Roger Green light, we'll intensify the pressure to draw their attention.
usm07_dial_0400: Green Light Team reporting, we have reached the dam. We'll start unpacking the special device.
usm07_dial_0401: The Doomsday Clock just started ticking! Move it, Commander!
usm07_dial_0402: Roger, Green Light. Report back when you’re ready for the final phase of the plan.
usm07_dial_0410: Green Light Team here, the device is in place and ready to go off. What are your orders?
usm07_dial_0411: Initiate the countdown and defend the bomb to the very end Captain.
usm07_dial_0420: Understood, we knew what we signed for. Godspeed!
usm07_dial_0421: Initiate the countdown and get the hell out of there, Captain. The bomb’s well hidden, no need to sacrifice yourself.
usm07_dial_0430: You won't have to repeat it twice! Alright boys, we need to move as if the hounds of hell were on our backs!
usm07_dial_0440: What are you doing here Commander? This place is about to blow up!
usm07_dial_0441: We’re aware! That's why we're here! The reinforcements I brought could very well save the day! If we succeed, there’ll be no need for that bomb to go off. Just give us a chance!
usm07_dial_0450: Understood, we'll cancel the arming procedure. But we stand by ready to reactivate it if you fail.
usm07_dial_0471: It's okay, we still have time. But don’t waste it!
usm07_dial_0472: Good, continue to hold your ground Commander. Poke at them if needed. Make sure they know you're here.
usm07_dial_0481: It’s getting dangerously tight, Commander! Please tell me you're gonna make it!
usm07_dial_0482: It’s working, they’re massing more and more troops by the river for their final assault against you.
usm07_dial_0491: You’ve never let me down, Commander, and now would be the worst possible time to start. Move it!
usm07_dial_0492: Hold tight, Commander! Don’t let them take the high ground! They will soon all be gone.
usm07_dial_0500: One minute left, soon the moment of truth.
usm07_dial_0501: No pressure, Commander! But maybe start hauling ass sometime today!
usm07_dial_0502: Oh and don't worry Commander, we’ll pin it on the Russians. We’ll call it an accident, a case of poor coordination, friendly fire, you name it.
usm07_dial_0510: Ten seconds left, see you on the other side I guess.
usm07_dial_0530: We’re getting slammed by ballistic missiles!
usm07_dial_0540: I've marked the coordinates of the launch, Commander.
rum01c_desc: Take control of the nuclear powerplant of Ignalina.
rum01c_task:
- Take control of the powerplant
- Defend until the engineers have cut the power
- Evacuate
rum01c_hint: It takes weeks to start up a nuclear power plant, whereas it only takes a few minutes to shut it down... if all goes well.
rum01c_cine_010: The Ignalina Nuclear Power Plant located right next to the frontier is a very vulnerable strategic asset. It supplies about 70% of Lithuania's electricity so shutting it down would strongly impact the Lithuanian resistance: TV and radio, cellphones, street cameras, everything would stop working.
rum01c_cine_020: The power plant is surrounded by a very swampy area which makes ground operations difficult to accomplish but this is not a problem for us: we have VDV units that can be transported by helicopter.
rum01c_cine_030: Shutting down a power plant is a matter of minutes while turning it back on takes weeks. So this is going to be a hit and run operation: we secure the perimeter, drop a team of assault engineers, they shut down the powerplant and everyone goes home before sunrise.
rum01c_brief_010: Captain Batalin, thank you for making yourself available at such short notice. This is much appreciated.
rum01c_brief_020: My pleasure agent Morozova, when the duty calls I'm always the first to answer.
rum01c_brief_030: Almost. Kommander, please meet Captain Batalin who will support you during this operation. He is one of our most brilliant young officers.
rum01c_brief_040: But, I thought you said I would be in command? I am perfectly capable of handling this whole operation on my own!
rum01c_brief_050: There is no doubt about your abilities Captain, but this is no longer an exercise, and one of you could be injured. So we must ensure there is redundancy in the chain of command.
rum01c_brief_060: Now let's get to work shall we?
rum01c_brief_070: The assault group tasked with securing the perimeter of the power plant will be brought first and escorted by attack helicopters.
rum01c_brief_080: The enemy resistance in the vicinity should be limited to the local reserve units. They have neither the equipment nor the training to face our airborne forces.
rum01c_brief_090: Once the perimeter is secured we send a second wave of helicopters carrying the engineers and additional reinforcements.
rum01c_brief_100: The activation of the emergency shutdown of the power plant should not take long. Once this is done we'll send the helicopter back to pick you up.
rum01c_brief_110: The engineers will be evacuated first, followed by your airborne troops. Hopefully you should be back before the enemy even has time to realize what is going on.
rum01c_intel_01:
VDV (Russian Airborne Forces)
They are organized as small specialized squads of 5 men each with a very distinct role and supported by airdroppable armored vehicles (BMD)
rum01c_intel_02:
VDV DSh (assault squad)
Equipped with rocket propelled grenade launchers (RPG), these units are deadly against vehicles at short range.
rum01c_intel_03:
VDV Spetsnaz (special forces)
Specialized in fighting enemy infantry at short range, they are equipped with suppressed weapons and small thermobaric rocket launchers.
rum01c_intel_04:
VDV Igla (air-defence)
Equipped with a short range anti-aircraft missile mostly efficient against helicopters and a PKP machine gun to offer medium range fire support against infantry.
rum01c_intel_05:
VDV AGS (automatic grenade launcher)
Equipped with two AGS-30 automatic grenade launchers they provide powerful long range support against infantry and light vehicles.
rum01c_intel_06:
VDV Metis (anti-tank missile)
Equipped with the Metis medium range anti-tank missile, they can deal with armored vehicles and even tanks if they come too close.
rum01c_intel_07:
BMD-2
A lightly armored transport vehicle equipped with a 30mm autocannon.
rum01c_intel_08:
Nona-S
A 120mm mortar mounted on a BMD chassis.
rum01c_intel_09:
Kornet-D1
Carries long range, high penetration, Kornet anti-tank guide missiles. Some of the missiles have a high explosive warhead instead to deal with infantry.
rum01c_hint_01_text: The reinforcements menu is now available. Your troops must be flown in by helicopter during this mission.
rum01c_task_hold_plant: Retain control of the power plant
rum01c_task_regroup: Regroup on the second line of defense
rum01c_task_fall_back: Fall back and standby for evacuation
rum01c_dial_0010: The powerplant is straight ahead, begin the attack, suppress all enemy resistance, and secure the landing zone!
rum01c_dial_0020: Here we go! Try to keep up with me Kommander, cause I won't wait for you! Давай!
rum01c_dial_0030: Logistics center clear for landing, resistance crushed!
rum01c_dial_0050: Patrols around the power plant eliminated! Keep up the good work!
rum01c_dial_0060: Batalin for HQ, all the landing zones are secured!
rum01c_dial_0061: Good job you two, continue to patrol the area and keep the landing zones safe. I'm sending in the transport helicopters.
rum01c_dial_0070: We've got boots on the ground in the logistics center!
rum01c_dial_0080: Our troops are taking over the railway station!
rum01c_dial_0090: The first wave has successfully landed.
rum01c_dial_0091: Our spetsnaz have stormed the power plant, not much resistance encountered but some workers have locked themselves behind the blast doors of the main control room.
rum01c_dial_0092: I'm sending the attack helicopters back to base immediately to escort the second wave containing the assault engineers.
rum01c_dial_0100: Defend the perimeter with what you have in the meantime, don't let anybody reach the power plant.
rum01c_dial_0110:
You heard her guys! Defend the perimeter and hold until the second wave arrives!
I hope I can count on you to help with the defense Kommander!
rum01c_dial_0120: Transport helicopters are also available to call reinforcements and bring supplies.
rum01c_dial_0130: The left flank is collapsing! Withdraw to our fallback positions!
rum01c_dial_0140: They squeezed us out of the railroad station! Fall back to the second line of defense!
rum01c_dial_0150: We're starting to run out of ammo down here, how much longer are we gonna need to hold on our own Lena?
rum01c_dial_0151: You've just lost an entire squad Kommander! We won't hold long if you're not more careful!
rum01c_dial_0152: Batalin for HQ, our losses are mounting fast, we won't hold on much longer.
rum01c_dial_0153: The helicopters have finished rearming and refueling and the engineers are on board. They will soon be on their way.
rum01c_dial_0154: The second wave is en route, hold tight!
rum01c_dial_0160: Enemy vehicles coming our way at the train station. Let's ambush them!
rum01c_dial_0170: Enemy convoy destroyed! We took prisoners, they are reservists from the local garrison which has been put on alert.
rum01c_dial_0180: Another convoy destroyed! We have captured an officer this time. According to what he said there is a large mechanized brigade stationed nearby and it has been alerted.
rum01c_dial_0181: It sounds like an attempt to frighten us but if this is true we need to be gone before they arrive or we won't see the morning.
rum01c_dial_0182: Don't worry, the Engineers will soon be here.
rum01c_dial_0190: Armored vehicles approaching the right flank! Fire at will, don't let them pass!
rum01c_dial_0200: Mechanized assault on the right flank destroyed! The rest of the infantry is falling back!
rum01c_dial_0210: Beware! Enemy vehicles are storming the left flank!
rum01c_dial_0220: The attack on the left flank has been repelled! Well done guys, keep it up!
rum01c_dial_0230: The enemy group on the left flank is retreating!
rum01c_dial_0240: Attention! Enemy armored vehicles are storming the left flank!
rum01c_dial_0250: Enemy's attack on the left flank repelled!
rum01c_dial_0260: Kommander, my guys report that they've observed enemy activity around us. They seems to be regrouping and preparing fire support positions.
rum01c_dial_0270: Their next assault will certainly be better prepared than their previous attempts and we might not be facing simple conscripts any more.
rum01c_dial_0280: It is getting hotter and hotter! Attention everyone! Replenish your ammunition and get ready for new attacks! I think we've pissed them off.
rum01c_dial_0290: Heads up! Enemy choppers! Grab a tube and shoot that bastard down!
rum01c_dial_0300: Fuck, I think they brought in some special forces! Don't let them land!
rum01c_dial_0310: One Black Hawk down! Good job, guys!
rum01c_dial_0320: A squad of engineers has been killed! This is a total failure! Abort the operation immediately and retreat!
rum01c_dial_0330: Kommander, the second wave transporting the assault engineers has arrived!
rum01c_dial_0340:
We have reached the blast doors of the control room.
20 centimetres of tempered steel and reinforced concrete, that's serious business!
rum01c_dial_0350: This might take a while. Keep the enemy as far away from the nuclear power plant as possible until they can access the control room and shut down both reactors.
rum01c_dial_0351: I'm sending a third wave of reinforcements with our reserves, they should arrive in a few minutes.
rum01c_dial_0360: We're under fire! What the hell is going on Kommander? If you think this is a good way of getting us to work faster, let me tell you that it is not!
rum01c_dial_0370: How long will it take? We have a full mechanized brigade coming our way and we won't be able to hold them for long.
rum01c_dial_0380:
[screeching metal sound]
We're in the same boat here. We're on it and we can't work faster, so we'll let you know when it's done!
rum01c_dial_0390: They're trying to overwhelm the right flank again! Spread out, fire at will!
rum01c_dial_0400: The right flank held its ground. Replenish your ammunition and prepare for the next attack!
rum01c_dial_0410: HQ to all ground units, the next wave is approaching the landing zone! Confirm visual.
rum01c_dial_0420: What the hell! They're flying towards the enemy! Break right, break right!
rum01c_dial_0430:
[beeping alarms sounds]
Roger that! Shit, there are tracers everywhere! Fuck, Stingers! Flares, flares! Take the landing party to the right, I'll cover!
rum01c_dial_0440: Sons of bitches, they shot down a helicopter!
rum01c_dial_0450: The last wave of paratroopers was dropped near the power plant. The helicopters are returning to base for repairs and will come back to support you as soon as possible! Good luck!
rum01c_dial_0460: Damn! Looks like we're left without air cover once again. Suck it up and drive on, I guess.
rum01c_dial_0470: Enemies attacking the left flank! Hold your positions! Give them hell!
rum01c_dial_0480: Enemy attack has failed! Well done, boys!
rum01c_dial_0490: Enemy choppers! You guys know what to do!
rum01c_dial_0500: We're starting to face armored vehicles with heavy weapons. These are Vilkas from the Iron Wolves brigade, one of the best trained Lithuanian mechanized units.
rum01c_dial_0510: Without armored vehicles of our own we won't be be able to resist much longer.
rum01c_dial_0520: Understood, we have a pair of Mi-26s available at the base. We can airlift you armored reinforcements. What's the most pressing need on the battlefield right now?
rum01c_dial_0530: Select the equipment to be carried by the Mi-26s
rum01c_dial_0540: Roger that. Reinforcements will be arriving shortly. Stand by for the package.
rum01c_dial_0545: The requested armored vehicles have been delivered! Good luck, guys!
rum01c_dial_0546: A Mi-26 has been shot down! We don't have replacements for these helicopters, so you'll have to make do without these vehicles.
rum01c_dial_0550: Attention: The situation in the area of operation is becoming more complicated.
rum01c_dial_0560: A column of Latvian armored vehicles has crossed the border and is moving in your direction. They should arrive on your right flank shortly.
rum01c_dial_0570: It's our lucky day apparently. Lithuania, Latvia, the only one missing is Estonia for a Baltic royal flush!
rum01c_dial_0580: Guys, get your RPGs ready! All eyes on the right flank! The fun's about to start there!
rum01c_dial_0590: One more grounded bird! Now that's what I call proper shooting!
rum01c_dial_0600: One more grounded bird! Good job, guys!
rum01c_dial_0610: Here comes the promised column of Latvians. Огонь!
rum01c_dial_0620: Oh shit, they brought mortars with them!
rum01c_dial_0630: I think we pushed the Latvians back. Well, that wasn't easy. It's too early to relax, we need to prepare for the next attack.
rum01c_dial_0640: Now they're coming in with mortars on the left flank! Hold on, boys!
rum01c_dial_0650: Their attack is also losing momentum on the left flank. That was close... damn mortars.
rum01c_dial_0660: Bloody hell, is it the Lithuanian-Latvian military cooperation celebration day? It looks like we're taking on both their entire armies in one fell swoop!
rum01c_dial_0670: What is the situation in the power plant? How much longer are we gonna have to hold these damn positions?
rum01c_dial_0680: We just breached into the control room and cleared it. It's only a matter of minutes now to trigger the automatic shutdown.
rum01c_dial_0690: Great news, we fire up the engines of the helicopters for the evacuation. Tell me when the shutdown is in effect and I'll send them.
rum01c_dial_0700: Engineers, have you heard? We need to evacuate now! Are you not done yet?!
rum01c_dial_0710: Not yet, the automatic shutdown failed. This damn equipment is rotten to the core! The control rods are stuck halfway through and refuse to go down.
rum01c_dial_0720: Are you joking me?! If you don't shut down the reactors in time, I swear we leave you here!
rum01c_dial_0730:
Listen, it's not my fault this place hasn't seen any maintenance since the days of Brezhnev!
Give us another 5 minutes, and we'll go to the reactor room to make the rods go down the hard way!
rum01c_dial_0740: What do you mean the hard way? Blowing this place up with us nearby is not part of the plan! Don't do anything stupid!
rum01c_dial_0750:
Well, we'll find out at the first kiss of my circular saw on these damn control rods!
Engineers out!
rum01c_dial_0760: The damn helicopters are coming again! Have they decided to land all of their special forces here?
rum01c_dial_0780: Damn, they're attacking on the right flank again! Hold your positions!
rum01c_dial_0790: All right, looks like all the armor's been destroyed.
rum01c_dial_0800: They're coming through on the left flank again. I guess they'll never learn. Fire at will, teach them a lesson!
rum01c_dial_0810: Great, I think we've smashed them all!
rum01c_dial_0820: I hear rotor blades again, more choppers? Just how many do they have?
rum01c_dial_0830: Great, I think we got it. This one won't get on my nerves today!
rum01c_dial_0840:
Things are calming down, did they finally give up?
That would surprise me, I don't like it.
rum01c_dial_0842: So that's what they were preparing! Fall back in the direction of the power plant! I'm sure they won't risk using heavy artillery near it!
rum01c_dial_0850: Attention, intelligence reports indicate that enemy heavy artillery is setting up in range of the power plant.
rum01c_dial_0851: I'm sure they won't risk using heavy artillery near the nuclear power plant. So stay near it to avoid being bombarded.
rum01c_dial_0860: It worked! First reactor has been shut down. We're moving to the second!
rum01c_dial_0870: Great work, the method is a bit unconventional but it's the result that matters. Carry on!
rum01c_dial_0880: My father used to tell me: If violence doesn't solve your problems, hit harder!
rum01c_dial_0890: Job done, we've shut down both reactors! We're moving to evac zone. Provide cover now!
rum01c_dial_0900:
What's this? Good news from you? I can't believe my ears!
Get ready to evacuate, we cover you.
rum01c_dial_0920: Let's hope they don't get lost this time! How hard is it to aim for the big building with the big pipes sticking out?!
rum01c_dial_0930: The engineers have been successfully evacuated.
rum01c_dial_0950: The engineers were killed during their evacuation. Well, at least they fulfilled their mission.
rum01c_dial_0960:
Batalin, prepare your troops, you go with the next wave.
Kommander, continue to defend the perimeter, you and your men will evacuate last.
rum01c_dial_0970: Shit, we're losing choppers! Evacuation zone is under enemy fire! Load up, quick, or we're all going to die here.
rum01c_dial_0980:
Your turn Kommander!
Bring all your remaining units to the evacuation zone.
Attack helicopters will protect your retreat.
rum01c_dial_1010: Time's up! Get the choppers back to base! All units that didn't evacuate in time, retreat to the forest and try to reach the border on foot!
rum01c_dial_1020: All the vehicles are been destroyed. Get the Mi-26 out of here.
rum01c_dial_1030: The last transport helicopters are arriving. The zone is hot so you will only have one minute to mount up!
rum01c_dial_1040: You heard her Kommander: one minute!
rum01c_dial_1050: We're losing too many choppers! All remaining cargo helos abort the evacuation and return to base immediately!
rum01c_dial_1060: Evacuation failed! All remaining ground units, retreat to the forest and try to reach the border on foot!
rum01c_dial_1070: The helicopters are in position! You've got one minute Kommander, hurry up!
rum01c_dial_1080: Send the Mi-26s back to base, there's no more equipment left to load. Kommander lost them all.
rum01c_dial_1090: Those Mi-26 are the biggest sitting ducks you can imagine, we cannot wait any longer and need to go! Now!
rum01c_dial_1100: All the infantry is here! Get in the choppers and let's head back to base!
rum01c_dial_1110: Our helicopters transporting the infantry have been shot down on their way back. This tragic event will add a painful scar to the memory of what could have been a glorious day for all of us. If it wasn't for the Kommander.
rum01c_dial_1120:
There's nothing left to evacuate, the mission is a success but the cost was high.
This tragic event will add a painful scar to the memory of what could have been a glorious day for all of us.
rum01c_dial_1130: They all got killed, get the Mi-8 out of here.
rum02c_desc: The russian advance is at a standstill due to a fuel shortage.
rum02c_task:
- Find supplies
- Grab them with transport helicopters
- Refund the helicopters containing supplies to accumulate resources
rum02c_hint: To capture supplies, move a unit inside of its radius of effect. Right clicking the label of enemy supplies order your unit to attack it.
rum02c_cine_010: Our troops have progressed rapidly through Lithuania giving us the opportunity for a pincer maneuver on Riga from the south.
rum02c_cine_020: They will never reach the city.
rum02c_cine_030: Oh really? And why is that Colonel?
rum02c_cine_040: I’ve read your plan, you’re a good student Agent Morozova, you have applied the manual to the letter and calculated the fuel requirement of this operation as instructed by the manual.
rum02c_cine_050: But the manual is wrong, and only experienced officers know that. In practice they will run out of fuel long before reaching the city.
rum02c_cine_060: Then I have a proposition Lena… I mean agent Morozova: our troops will pass near Mažeikiai oil refinery. We could raid it and take fuel for our troops, what do you think?
rum02c_cine_070: Mmh, I like your audacity, Batalin. You should take an example from him, Colonel.
rum02c_cine_080: The Mi-26 is the largest helicopter ever built with a carrying capacity of 20 tons. They could carry troops and armored vehicles on the outward journey and fuel on the return trip.
rum02c_cine_090: I love your plan, let's put it into practice!
rum02c_brief_010: As I predicted, the first vehicles start to run out of fuel.
rum02c_brief_020: Batalin, order the column to stop immediately. Gather what little fuel we have left and top off the helicopters.
rum02c_brief_030: But our vehicles will be sitting ducks if you do that!
rum02c_brief_040: Don't worry Colonel, we'll be back in no time. Stay here and make sure everything's ready for our return.
rum02c_brief_050: Kommander, prepare a light assault force that can be transported by Mi-26, the oil refinery will certainly be defended.
rum02c_brief_060: It is located at the frontier between Lithuania and Latvia and close enough from the sea to receive reinforcements from the USMC units which have landed on the coast.
rum02c_brief_070: You'd better hurry up, get what we need and get the hell out of there before they all come down on you.
rum02c_brief_080: Speaking of which, what kind of fuel do we need for our vehicles?
rum02c_brief_090: Anything! That's the magic of multi-fuel engines: you can pee in them after a binge and they'll start right up. Haha!
rum02c_brief_100: Speaking from experience, I suppose. Very well, then. We'll take everything we can find.
rum02c_intel_01:
Mi-26
A giant cargo helicopter able to lift up to 20 tons which is enough for an armored vehicle.
rum02c_hint_01_text: By selling a Mi-26 full of supplies you get 90% of the helicopter's value back, plus up to 200 for the supplies it carries.
rum02c_hint_02_text: Keep some reinforcement points in reserve to call in new Mi-26s. The resources they grab is your only source of revenue.
rum02c_hint_03_text: You can now call in airplanes to support your helicopters.
rum02c_task_yard: Take control of the marshalling yard
rum02c_task_loadfuel: Load the fuel in the helicopters
rum02c_task_refund: Send the fuel back to base
rum02c_task_arty: Find and destroy the heavy artillery
rum02c_medal_1_c2: Unlock all the units from Captain Batalin
rum02c_medal_3_c2: Complete the mission in less than 45 minutes
rum02c_dial_0010: Kommander, your group arrived first, begin the operation immediately. Batalin group will soon follow.
rum02c_dial_0020: Your primary task is to conduct a reconnaissance in force and secure this first fuel depot near the marshalling yard.
rum02c_dial_0030: We need these supplies, so watch your fire!
rum02c_dial_0040: Hurry up Kommander, capture this fuel depot while the surprise effect is still on our side and the enemy has not mobilized their reserves.
rum02c_dial_0050: Good job, the marshalling yard is under our control. Protect the fuel until the Mi-26s arrive.
rum02c_dial_0060: You have lost control of the captured fuel. This is a complete failure, retreat immediately!
rum02c_dial_0070: The Mi-26 helicopters are here. Grab the supplies and send them back to base.
rum02c_dial_0071: The more resources you send back to base, the more units can be resupplied and made available to you.
rum02c_dial_0080: The fuel has been received, several helicopters have been resupplied, you can call them as reinforcement Kommander.
rum02c_dial_0081: Don't forget to keep a reserve to call in additional Mi-26s. They are needed to grab more resources.
rum02c_dial_0090: Commander, a Mi-26 has been shot down! I'm transferring one from Batalin's group to get you out of this situation.
rum02c_dial_0091:
What?!
Kommander stop screwing up! I'm the one fixing your shit!
rum02c_dial_0092: That's enough Kommander! Regain your quarters, we will continue this mission without you.
rum02c_dial_0100: Excellent work, all the fuel has been loaded in the helicopters. Now send them back to base.
rum02c_dial_0101: We received the fuel and were able to fill the tanks of several helicopters. You can call them as reinforcements Kommander.
rum02c_dial_0110: But we need much more than that, your next target is the oil refinery itself which contains much more resources than this small depot.
rum02c_dial_0115: If you have time look for additional supply dumps you could grab in the nearby warehouses.
rum02c_dial_0120: Great, you've found some fuel, but it's not enough. There is much more stored at the marshalling yard.
rum02c_dial_0130: You found some more fuel. Get a Mi-26 to load it and send it to the rear.
rum02c_dial_0140: Batalin here, sorry I'm late Kommander. Unfortunately, my helicopters ran out of fuel before reaching the area of operation and we had to continue on the ground.
rum02c_dial_0150: If you could help me out by sending fuel to the area indicated, I could provide you with additional reinforcements.
rum02c_dial_0160: So the brilliant Kommander managed to spare a bit of his time and resources for the poor Batalin. That's a surprise I have to admit.
rum02c_dial_0161: We have refueled some helicopters for you, you can call them as reinforcements.
rum02c_dial_0162: Keep sending me fuel, I need more to resupply the rest of my battlegroup.
rum02c_dial_0163: Another fuel delivery from the magnanimous Kommander? It's my lucky day apparently.
rum02c_dial_0164: I guess you're only intersted in getting more of my helicopters am I right? Well take them, I don't care as long as you keep delivering fuel for my guys.
rum02c_dial_0165: More fuel? Well, that very generous of you Kommander. Maybe I was wrong about you after all.
rum02c_dial_0166: I've transferred the best helicopters I have under your command, you can call them in as reinforcements. These are beautiful machines, take good care of them please.
rum02c_dial_0170: Kommander, I'm launching a flanking attack! Support my troops with your helicopters and this objective will soon be ours.
rum02c_dial_0180: The fuel depot is in our hands, but unfortunately it's not even half full. We'll have to venture further into the refinery to find more.
rum02c_dial_0181: For the moment grab everything you can here and send it back to base. And establish a defensive perimeter to secure the loading operations.
rum02c_dial_0190: We're consuming half of the supplies we manage to capture, we have no choice, we need to find more and faster.
rum02c_dial_0200: Our next targets will be the processing plant and the loading platform. These areas should be littered with fuel dumps.
rum02c_dial_0210: The processing plant is clear, begin the loading operations, watch out for counter attacks.
rum02c_dial_0220: The loading platform has been secured, send in the Mi-26s, defend the perimeter.
rum02c_dial_0280:
Both objectives are under out control.
Your next goal is to cross the highway and attack the remaining production facilities.
rum02c_dial_0281: They are well defended but there should be more than enough fuel stored there.
rum02c_dial_0290: They are starting to evacuate fuel from the production facilities with trucks. Stop them Kommander!
rum02c_dial_0311: Ha! Nice fireworks, Kommander! Heavy artillery destroyed.
rum02c_dial_0312: They opened fire with heavy artillery on the fuel depots! This is scrorched earth tactics! They will burn the place down if we don't stop them!
rum02c_dial_0320: We blocked the highway on both sides. Let those little trucks come to us!
rum02c_dial_0340: Some enemy fuel trucks went through. I guess the task was too hard for you Komander. Unarmed trucks agains attack helicopters, what could you have done?!
rum02c_dial_0350: That's enough Batalin. Kommander we have received reports of of a strong enemy counter attack Involving Latvian, Lithuanian and American troops.
rum02c_dial_0360: I'm putting Su-25s ground attack planes under your command. Use them to crush entrenched positions and to counter large enemy formations.
rum02c_dial_0370: It looks like you've destroyed all the enemy trucks Kommander. It wasn't that difficult, was it? Attack helicopters against unarmed trucks.
rum02c_dial_0382: Enemy fuel trucks detected, give them hell!
rum02c_dial_0390: Perfect, we have accumulated enough resources to resupply our troops heading towards Riga.
rum02c_dial_0400: Mission accomplished, let's get out of here before the American air force falls upon us.
rum02c_dial_0410: USMC helicopters are arriving in force, so get ready for them!
rum02c_dial_0420: Another wave of Marines helicopter spotted on our radars, they're coming fast!
rum02c_dial_0430: Sukois are patroling in high altitude to keep you safe from enemy aircraft, but you need to hurry, they cannot loiter indefinitely.
rum02c_dial_0440: Our high altitude fighters have spotted enemy planes heading their way, they will soon engage each other.
rum02c_dial_0450: You need to speed up the process and gather resources faster!
rum02c_dial_0460: The high altitude furball is going down, stay clear of enemy aircraft! Don't take a stray missile!
rum02c_dial_0470: They are gone for now, but our planes need to return to base. You will be defenseless in case of a new attack.
rum02c_dial_0480: Finish gathering the resources we need before the enemy planes come back or you're done for!
rum02c_dial_0490: Enemy airplanes are coming back! It's impossible to continue this operation in a contested airspace, abort, I repeat, abort!
rum03c_desc: Russian troops have reached the city of Jelgava but lost their commander.
rum03c_task:
- Re-establish contact and regroup with our dispersed elements inside the city.
- Take full control of the city
rum03c_hint: Urban combat is unforgiving. Constantly obstructed lines of sight limit fire support effectiveness, and every corner or building could conceal a threat.
rum03c_cine_010: The fuel collected at the refinery enabled Batalin to reach Jelgava. It is the last city on our way before Riga.
rum03c_cine_020: The situation there seems to be a complete mess, I wasn’t able to reach anyone over the radio for the past 12 hours.
rum03c_cine_030: A recent drone reconnaissance is showing heavy artillery shelling and close quarter combat on the western bank but there is no clear frontline and it’s hard to distinguish who is who.
rum03c_cine_040: Colonel, take the Kommander with you. Re-establish cohesion between our troops and resume the assault. I want this city taken and the road to Riga opened without further delay.
rum03c_cine_050: Damn, it feels like Grozny all over again...
rum03c_cine_060: Let's hope there is still someone to save this time.
rum03c_brief_010: There is a small airfield near the city, we will establish a command post there.
rum03c_brief_020: Batalin you will defend the perimeter while the Komander conducts the first reconnaissance.
rum03c_brief_030: Sir, why am I always kept in reserve? How am I supposed to distinguish myself if I’m always given ungrateful tasks?!
rum03c_brief_040: You are young and impulsive Batalin, such temper can cost the life of many men.
rum03c_brief_050: But I want to be like you, a war hero! There are books about you at the academy, your men called you “the man who didn’t fear the bullets”. I would like my name to impose respect too.
rum03c_brief_060: I don’t come from a rich family, I had to compete all my life with the “sons of someone” and work twice harder to prove myself.
rum03c_brief_070: Forget this name “the man who didn’t fear the bullets”! You have no idea of the cost that comes with such a name!
rum03c_brief_080: So is that why I am always a second choice to you? I thought you would be different and that you would be able to see beyond names and origins.
rum03c_brief_090: I don't care about your name or who the fuck your mother and father are, Batalin! Now, go and prepare your troops for the task I've assigned you!
rum03c_brief_110: What happens Colonel, you seem upset, has a ghost of the past come to haunt you?
rum03c_brief_120: Nothing you shouldn't already know, as I'm sure you've done a background check on me.
rum03c_brief_130: Now if you excuse me I have another city to conquer apparently.
rum03c_intel_01:
UR-77 Meteorit
A self-propelled mine-clearing rocket launcher. Can be used against entrenched infantry with devastating effect.
rum03c_intel_02:
Mechanized platoon
A tactical unit composed of BMP-1PG infantry fighting vehicles and squads of Motostrelki, Pulemetchiki and Fagot anti-tank missiles.
rum03c_intel_03:
Anti-Aircraft Platoon
A tactical unit that includes MT-LB 6MB5 armored vehicles equipped with anti-aircraft guns and missiles as well as infantry equipped with Igla anti-aircraft missiles.
rum03c_intel_04:
Assault Platoon
A tactical unit that includes BMP-2G infantry fighting vehicles with Shturmoviki assault squads
rum03c_intel_05:
Tank Platoon
A tactical unit consisting of T-72BA tanks modernized with "Kontakt-5" explosive reactive armor and upgraded engines.
rum03c_intel_06:
Artillery Platoon
A group of 2S3M1 Akatsia-M1 152 mm howitzers.
rum03c_intel_07:
Reconnaissance Platoon
A tactical unit consisting of sniper squads and Razvedla squads.
rum03c_intel_08:
Flamethrower Platoon
A tactical unit that includes armored BTO fire support vehicles and Ognemetchiki flamethrower squads.
rum03c_medal_3_condition: Capture the city in less than 40 minutes
rum03c_hint_01_text: The amount of resources required to recover platoons is indicated when place your cursor on the label.
rum03c_hint_02_text: Combat aviation is now available as reinforcements.
rum03c_task_find_batalin: Link up with the troops stranded in the city
rum03c_task_find_squads: Find most of the stranded platoons
rum03c_obj_scout_find: Scout the area | Find allied troops
rum03c_dial_0010: Kommander, you're just in time. Half an hour ago, Batalin went on a reconnaissance mission we lost communication.
rum03c_dial_0020: I hope he's all right... Do some reconnaissance of your own, find Batalin and find out what's going on in this damn city.
rum03c_dial_0030: Guys, there's a car approaching from the rear. Hold your fire! Kommander, is that you? Be careful, don't go any further, the enemy controls all the vantage points!
rum03c_dial_0040: Those bastards won't let us raise our heads. I've organized a defensive position with the scattered troops I found on the outskirts of town, but the situation is dire.
rum03c_dial_0050: I can't re-establish contact with the rest of our troops, the HQ was hit hard by artillery yesterday and we have lost all contact since then.
rum03c_dial_0060: The enemy is constantly sneaking up on us with snipers and anti-tank missiles. We're reduced to using mine-clearing vehicles as makeshift artillery to repel them.
rum03c_dial_0070: Understood. Batalin, you have done enough, go to the base and wait for further instructions. The Kommander will take over from here.
rum03c_dial_0080: I'm sending you transport helicopters with supplies. Use your trucks to dispatch them to our troops. Restore their cohesion and rally them under your command.
rum03c_dial_0090: Act quickly, we need to reconnect with our troops on the other side of the city as soon as possible.
rum03c_dial_0110: Supplies delivered, the first mechanized platoon is now under your command.
rum03c_dial_0120: You lost your recon units too Kommander! блядь! Are all my subordinates equally incapable of conducting reconnaissance?!
rum03c_dial_0130: The second mechanized platoon has been resupplied and is now under your command.
rum03c_dial_0140: All units are patched up and ready to fight!
rum03c_dial_0160: We've got this high-riser under our control, move on.
rum03c_dial_0180: Alright, all clear. Now we can start regrouping with the rest of our guys scattered in the city.
rum03c_dial_0190: Establish defenses in front of the main bridge to prevent any further enemy breakthrough while you locate and rally as many units as possible. We need to put together a large strike for our offensive.
rum03c_dial_0200: I sent helicopters transporting supplies and recon units, use them to find and rally our scattered units. Resources are limited, so spend them wisely.
rum03c_dial_0210: Once you estimate that you have gathered enough forces, attack the castle. Apparently US marines have established defensive positions there controlling the bridge.
rum03c_dial_0220: Kommander, you found another mechanized platoon. Bring them supplies if you want to rally them.
rum03c_dial_0230: One more mechanized platoon under your command. As practice shows, you can never have too much infantry.
rum03c_dial_0240: You've re-established contact with another infantry unit.
rum03c_dial_0250: An additional Mechanized platoon back in action. These were operating with a reconnaissance platoon. The last know position of the scouts is in a village on the other side of the river.
rum03c_dial_0251: We manged to re-establish radio communication with them, they are now under your command.
rum03c_dial_0260: Look! A platoon of flamethrower infantry. These guys are experts at storming forests and city blocks with their incendiary rockets.
rum03c_dial_0270: The God of fire has found its preachers! The flamethrower platoon is under your command.
rum03c_dial_0280: Kommander, you've found a platoon of Sturmoviki. There's nothing like assault infantry for urban combat! Get them the supplies they need to rally them.
rum03c_dial_0290: Sturmoviki resupplied and ready for the assault!
rum03c_dial_0300: The anti-aircraft platoon survived. Get them supplies, these guys are gonna be very useful against USMC choppers.
rum03c_dial_0310: Now it is clear why our guys are hiding. All the high buildings are controlled by the enemy, any attempt to move is met by an anti-tank missile or sniper bullet.
rum03c_dial_0320: The anti-aircraft platoon is back in formation and ready to carry out your orders.
rum03c_dial_0330: Look! It's our heavy artillery. It seems pretty intact but they are almost out of ammunition. It will require quite a lot of supplies to get them back into action.
rum03c_dial_0340: The howitzers are reloaded and ready to support us with long range fire.
rum03c_dial_0350: Kommander, we've re-established contact with a tank platoon. They appear to be our last surviving tanks and they are in poor condition. It will require quite a lof of supplies to bring them back into action.
rum03c_dial_0360: Kommander, the tank platoon is ready to carry out your orders. Be careful with all those anti-tank missiles lurking around.
rum03c_dial_0370: Kommander, a group of scouts and snipers have re-established contact. They found themselves isolated by the enemy counter-offensive after crossing the river.
rum03c_dial_0380: Kommander, you found our reconnaissance team. These guys lost contact with headquarters during the enemy counterattack.
rum03c_dial_0390: You found another mechanized platoon. Apparently, the battle left them relatively unscathed. They are immediately ready for battle.
rum03c_dial_0400: A second tank platoon! They ran into mines and were immobilized, but the crews survived.
rum03c_dial_0410: I'm sending in a helicopter with supplies to make field repairs and get them back into service.
rum03c_dial_0420: The Mi-26 carrying the supplies was shot down. The city is still partially controlled by the enemy. I cannot risk another helicopter so you'll have to make do with your trucks.
rum03c_dial_0430: An enemy convoy is crossing the main bridge! They have supply trucks with them. They probably thought the bridge was still under their control!
rum03c_dial_0440: They are attacking across the main bridge again! They know we're here this time!
rum03c_dial_0450: The remnants of the enemy attack are retreating. Good work, Kommander. But it's too early to relax, now they'll start reinforcing the defenses on their side of the river.
rum03c_dial_0460: We need to hurry. Gather more troops for the offensive. I have also requested air support, several bombers stand ready to pave the way for our ground offensive.
rum03c_dial_0470: You rallied enough units to start the offensive. I'm sure there are more you didn't find, but time is running out. Intelligence reports that USMC reinforcements are on their way from Riga, we can't wait any longer.
rum03c_dial_0480: Triangulation of enemy radio signals allowed us to determine the location of their HQ somewhere around those high-rises
rum03c_dial_0490: Your mission is to cross the river and attack the enemy's headquarters. Batalin will attack parallel to you, across the bridge on your left flank.
rum03c_dial_0500: Su-24 bombers and Su-25 tank killers support is available. I have notified them and they are now operating under your command.
rum03c_dial_0510: Colonel, the reserves have arrived. I'm launching the attack on the flank now!
rum03c_dial_0520: Wait! The right flank is not yet ready to attack, they just regained combat readiness and are not yet in battle formation!
rum03c_dial_0530: Too late Colonel, they certainly spotted us already, any delay would be fatal. Let's go guys, to battle, Давай!!
rum03c_dial_0540: Batalin! Damn you! ... Okay... Sokol 1, Sokol 2, requesting airstrikes at these coordinates. Level those buildings other side of that bridge!
rum03c_dial_0550: Batalin, your entire flamethrower platoon is dead!
rum03c_dial_0551: I can see that on my own thank you Sir! We're fucking busy down here!
rum03c_dial_0552: Damn it, Batalin! All your infantry is dead. Take cover wherever you can and wait for reinforcements.
rum03c_dial_0553: Shit! Attention, to all surviving infantry, take cover! We've drawn enough attention to us, now would be the moment to make your move Kommander!
rum03c_dial_0554: Batalin, your whole tank platoon is on fire! Stop this slaughter and fall back!
rum03c_dial_0555: There's a goddamn battle going on Colonel, casualties are to be expected! The men are fighting heroically! We're diverting a significant portion of the enemy defenses! Push your attack now Kommander!
rum03c_dial_0556: Colonel, we reached the outskirts of the city! My guys will hold these positions. That was fucking heroic! Now it's your turn to prove yourself Kommander!
rum03c_dial_0557: Roger that Batalin! Commander, don't lag behind, push on your side as well!
rum03c_dial_0560: Beware Kommander, U.S. Marines helicopters inbound! You crossed the river just in time. To be caught during the crossing with your pants down would have been a massacre!
rum03c_dial_0570: Excellent, the enemy headquarters has been destroyed. The remnants of the enemy forces are retreating!
rum03c_dial_0580: Черт, surface to air missiles firing from the city! We received the approximate coordinates from the pilots. Destroy them at the first opportunity before we lose all the air power we've been allocated.
rum03c_dial_0600: Good job Kommander, enemy air defense eliminated.
rum03c_dial_0610: The skies are clear again for our air force.
rum03c_dial_0620: Kommander, the 1st Mechanized Platoon has been completely eliminated!
rum03c_dial_0630: Kommander, 2nd Mechanized Platoon was wiped out!
rum03c_dial_0640: Kommander, the entire 3rd Mechanized Platoon is lost!
rum03c_dial_0650: Kommander, you've lost your entire flamethrower platoon!
rum03c_dial_0660: Kommander, the Sturmoviki platoon is not responding, they're all dead.
rum03c_dial_0670: The 1st tank platoon is no more Kommander. That's a major blow!
rum03c_dial_0680: The anti-aircraft platoon is decimated, let's hope they won't bring too much helicopters!
rum03c_dial_0690: Kommander, you've lost the 4th Mechanized Platoon. Move your infantry back and resupply it before it's completely wiped out!
rum03c_dial_0700: Kommander, you've lost 5th Mechanized Platoon. At this rate you will soon run out of infantry!
rum03c_dial_0710: Cancel the assault Kommander! You've lost too many units, this is a slaughter! I have no choice but to pause the attack and take several days to regroup of forces. This is a complete failure!
rum03c_dial_0720: Блядь, here comes the enemy artillery! We need to destroy it rapidly before our losses mount up. Their approximate coordinates have been marked, you know what to do!
rum03c_dial_0730: One artillery battery destroyed, keep up the good work Kommander!
rum03c_dial_0740: The enemy artillery has been silenced! Отлично!
rum03c_dial_0750: You're almost there Kommander! Not a step back! Capture the enemy HQ! They're pulling back their last forces to defend it!
rum04c_desc: Take control of Riga's airport with airdropped VDV.
rum04c_task:
- Clear the air space
- Drop airborne troops
- Secure the airport
rum04c_hint: Cargo planes need a long preparation time before dropping troops. If you give the order at the last moment, the plane will have to make a loop.
rum04c_cine_010: The Latvian plan is clear now, they concentrated the majority of their defense around their capital city of Riga where our supply lines would be stretched and force us into a long battle of attrition while partisans raid our rear lines.
rum04c_cine_020: The Iskander cruise missiles we have set up in Parnu are preventing the city from being resupplied by sea.
rum04c_cine_030: But they can still be resupplied by air from the international airport located right next to the city.
rum04c_cine_040: Then we will take this airport with our airborne troops.
rum04c_cine_050: Our ground forces will exert maximum pressure on the defenses to fix as many enemy units as possible on the front line, while our paratroopers will jump onto the airport and prevent any new enemy aircraft from landing.
rum04c_cine_060: Once they are cut from their last line of supplies the Latvian defences will not hold for long.
rum04c_brief_010: Airborne operations are always extremely risky. A single plane shot down and it’s a hundred lost souls all at once.
rum04c_brief_020: It would be much safer to wait for reinforcements and secure our supply line…
rum04c_brief_030: Are you a war hero Colonel?
rum04c_brief_040: Am I… Yes of course, I earned the distinction, but…
rum04c_brief_050: What about you Captain Batalin? Would you like to become one?
rum04c_brief_070: This operation will require all your expertise combined, so you will all be part of the assault, that includes you Colonel.
rum04c_brief_080: You want me to jump from a plane? At my age? Are you trying to kill me?!
rum04c_brief_090: You will be airdropped directly in your command vehicle. This tactic has been experimented and will be necessary to be immediately combat ready after the drop.
rum04c_brief_100: We will proceed in 3 waves, Kommander you will be the first, followed by Batalin, and finally you Colonel.
rum04c_brief_110: So as you can see I’m taking good care of you, by the time you arrive the zone should already be secured.
rum04c_intel_01:
R-37
Long range air-to-air missile. Its powerful warhead deals massive damage to enemy aircraft, and its extended range allows you to engage targets from a safe distance.
rum04c_intel_02:
R-77
Medium range air-to-air missile. Your primary weapon for engaging enemy aircraft.
rum04c_intel_03:
R-73
Short range air-to-air missile. Also effective against enemy helicopters.
rum04c_hint_01_text: Call in fighter jets to gain air superiority.
rum04c_hint_02_text: Drop a maximum of troops while the air space is ours. The reinforcement point you don't use will be lost.
rum04c_hint_03_text: Su-25 tank hunter planes are available to support you
rum04c_task_seczones: Hold the zones beyond the runway
rum04c_medal_1_c1: Finish the mission in any difficulty level
rum04c_medal_1_c2: Do not lose control of any zone
rum04c_medal_2_c1: Finish the mission in medium difficulty or higher
rum04c_medal_3_c1: Finish the mission in hard difficulty
rum04c_medal_3_c2: Complete the mission without using BMD-4M or Sprut-SD
rum04c_cut_010: We're entering their radar bubble, stay tight!
rum04c_cut_012: Accelerate to attack speed. This is it, boys!
rum04c_dial_0010: Riga airport is in sight, begin the air sweep!
rum04c_dial_0020: Kommander, they need backup! Where are your planes?!
rum04c_dial_0050: Enemy bird down, keep up the good work!
rum04c_dial_0060: The enemy planes are retreating; the sky is ours for the moment. But this is just a respite, they will come back.
rum04c_dial_0070: The first window of opportunity for a drop is open! Send in the first wave of paratroopers, Kommander. Drop everything you can, while you can!
rum04c_dial_0080: According to our intel, the airport itself is very lightly defended, the attack by our mechanized formations has drawn the defenders' attention elsewhere.
rum04c_dial_0090: Our fighters will soon need to refuel. The window of opportunity for the airdrop is closing fast. Drop everything you can NOW, Kommander!
rum04c_dial_0100: It's too late, the fighters must return to base.
rum04c_dial_0110: Why didn't you drop anything, Kommander?! We must cancel this operation!
rum04c_dial_0120: The number of troops you dropped will never be sufficient to hold such a large airport. I'm canceling this operation before it becomes a disaster.
rum04c_dial_0130: The fighters must return to base; the airdrop window is now closed.
rum04c_dial_0140: Alright, you've got boots on the ground. Reorganize your troops and and begin engaging the airport's security forces.
rum04c_dial_0150: They're retreating! Now, establish a first defensive perimeter while waiting for the second wave of paratroopers.
rum04c_dial_0160: Hurry up, capture the designated areas and establish defensive positions, Kommander!
rum04c_dial_0170: We've detected enemy groups moving in your direction to retake the airport. We can't send the next waves unless the drop zone is not secured.
rum04c_dial_0180: So keep these positions under your control and repel the enemy assaults until the second wave arrives.
rum04c_dial_0190: So make sure these positions are under your control by the time our planes return or I will have to cancel the operation.
rum04c_dial_0200: One of our positions has been overwhelmed, recapture it, Kommander!
rum04c_dial_0210: We lost control of the airport, we must cancel the operation!
rum04c_dial_0220: They are falling back to regroup apparently, that was close!
rum04c_dial_0230: Excellent, the first attack has been repelled.
rum04c_dial_0240: Our planes have returned for the second drop.
rum04c_dial_0250: Sokol-1 the skies are clear, nothing on our radars, send in the transports!
rum04c_dial_0260: Perfect, Kommander, call in your reinforcements before the end of this second airdrop window.
rum04c_dial_0270: Batalin, you will defend the Western side of the airstrip while the Kommander expands our defensive perimeter to the rest of the airport.
rum04c_dial_0280: Никто, кроме нас! Давай! There will be medals for everyone after this mission!
rum04c_dial_0290: Don't die for a medal today, Batalin! Stay focused!
rum04c_dial_0300: What about your medals Colonel? How many died for you to get them?
rum04c_dial_0310: What do you want from me in the end, Agent Morozova?! I earned my medals serving my country!
rum04c_dial_0320: Liar! Those memoirs you present as your own were written by my father — First Lieutenant Igor Morozov, who served under your command in Chechnya!
rum04c_dial_0330: He sent me a copy before you sent him to his death, and I recognize each and every one of his words in those memoirs you claim as yours.
rum04c_dial_0340: Is that true Colonel? All these stories, all your medals, just a big lie?
rum04c_dial_0350: Things are not as simple as that! I'll explain myself but we have more urgent matters! Enemy counter attacks are coming your way!
rum04c_dial_0360: One position has been overwhelmed, recapture it immediately, Kommander!
rum04c_dial_0370: I'm holding my line Kommander, can't you do the same?! Don't let them hit me in the back!
rum04c_dial_0380: They are falling back again, let's hope it's for good this time. Send in the last wave of reinforcements!
rum04c_dial_0390: Sokol-1, still nothing on radar, you can proceed!
rum04c_dial_0400: Colonel, it's time. Time to jump and act like the hero you claim to be!
rum04c_dial_0410: Sokol-1, I'm being lit up! There's nothing on radar!
rum04c_dial_0430: [Static] Do you copy? I hard landed outside the drop zone! I'm alone here, do you copy? Aaah! I can't move, I've broken my leg and my vehicle's down.
rum04c_dial_0440: I see the smoke of the crash, Comrade Colonel. I’m moving my forces to assist you, hold on!
rum04c_dial_0450: Leave him, Captain. Let him die like he left my father to die in Grozny. He's as good as dead anyway. Enemy troops are converging on his position.
rum04c_dial_0460: That's a negative Lena, I'm going in.
rum04c_dial_0470: This imposter is not worth saving, he is not the hero you think he is.
rum04c_dial_0480: Every man is worth saving, and that's what your father wrote in his memoirs. And I'll honor him with my actions.
rum04c_dial_0490: Kommander, take command of my men and hold the airport until I come back!
rum04c_dial_0510: Batalin? I didn't think you would be the one to come... I guess I've always underestimated you.
rum04c_dial_0520: You may be a liar and a disgrace, but since you're not "the man who doesn't fear the bullets" ready to risk it all for his comrades, then I’ll be that man instead.
rum04c_dial_0530: That man died a long time ago...
rum04c_dial_0540: Okay, your vehicle's up and running again, fall back to the airfield, we’ll cover you!
rum04c_dial_0550: I'm safe now. Batalin, fall back to the airport. You've done enough, you've got nothing to prove anymore, don't push your chances!
rum04c_dial_0570: Fuck my vehicle is hit! We're immobilized! They're arriving from every direction! [Static]
rum04c_dial_0590: Блядь! Kommander, we lost Batalin. I'm coming back to the airport with a large formation of US armored units on my heels.
rum04c_dial_0600: Shift your defense in this direction, don't let them retake the airport! Our first ground forces are arriving to relieve you, hold until they arrive!
rum04c_dial_0610: Abandon the two western defensive positions across the landing strip if needed to concentrate your defense on the terminal of the airport.
rum04c_dial_0620: We've lost one of defensive positions accross the landing strip!
rum04c_dial_0630: We've lost our positions accross the landing strip, focus your defense around the terminal of the airport! Hold the line!
rum04c_dial_0640: We've lost the main terminal! Scatter your troops in the nearby wooden area, we have no choice but to concede the airport!
rum04c_dial_0650: Our fighters have regained the air superiority, you can call in new reinforcements and strike planes!
rum04c_dial_0660: Our ground forces have finally linked up with us. The surviving US forces are surrendering and Riga will soon follow. That was a costly but brilliant victory today, Kommander.
rum01n_desc: We cross the border at Narva
rum01n_hint: The river Narva marks the frontier between Russia and Estonia. The city cut by the river is called Narva on the Estonian side and Ivangorod on the Russian side.
rum01n_cine_030: Доброе утро Colonel Demidov, this is agent Lena Morozova from SVR.
rum01n_cine_040: Huh? Secret service? What is it about?
rum01n_cine_050: Duty calls Colonel. Mother Russia once again requires your services. We are launching a series of military operations in the Baltic States in retaliation to the attack of our troops escorting supplies to Kaliningrad.
rum01n_cine_060: But… I was supposed to retire in…
rum01n_cine_090: Then this is understood. You will work under my supervision in a special mobile command unit. Pack up your things, a helicopter is already on its way to pick you up.
rum01n_cine_100: But… but where are you taking me?
rum01n_cine_110: Grab something Warm, we’re going to Narva.
rum01n_brief_010: Colonel Demidov, finally you are here. Please meet Captain Batalin.
rum01n_brief_020: It’s an honor sir, I've read your memoirs at the academy!
rum01n_brief_030: Ah? Yes, I've read them too…
rum01n_brief_040: And did you have time to read the documents I left for you in the helicopter during the flight?
rum01n_brief_050: Well yes, the general intention is clear but there are plenty of elements that need reviewing, starting with a proper inventory of our forces.
rum01n_brief_060: That’s why you are here Colonel, your expertise is highly valued and I'm sure you will rapidly find solutions to all those details.
rum01n_brief_070: You will also be assisted in your task by the Kommander, who will handle the direct command of our troops and who will, I’m sure, always make the most of every situation.
rum01n_brief_080: Now let’s get out into the field and start this inventory.
rum01n_intel_01:
Shturmoviki
Motorized infantry assault squads specialized in anti-infantry combat. Armed with long-range RPG-7s loaded with HE grenades.
rum01n_intel_02:
Pulemetchiki
Support squad equipped with three PKP machine guns and a couple of RPGs to defend against light vehicles. Mostly a defensive squad which cannot fire its machine guns on the move.
rum01n_intel_03:
Eesti Kaitseliit (reservists)
Reservists of the Estonian Defence League, hastily mobilized. Armed with Carl Gustav launchers, capable of engaging light to medium armor.
rum01n_intel_04:
Jalaväe Jagu (regular infantry)
Core infantry of the Estonian Armed Forces. Better trained and larger squads than reservist units.
rum01n_intel_05:
Eesti Scouts
Estonian reconnaissance units. Highly trained and equipped with short-range Spike SR anti-tank missiles.
rum01n_intel_06:
XA-180
Main APC of the Estonian military. Lightly armored but fast.
rum01n_intel_07:
CV-9035
Armored IFV armed with a powerful autocannon. A serious threat to any lightly armored vehicle.
rum01n_hint_01_text: Move a crew to a vehicle you want to control.
rum01n_hint_02_text: Mechanics will gradually restore damaged vehicles and deploy them at your base. Check back there if you’re taking losses.
rum01n_hint_03_text: If your infantry takes casualties, trucks will periodically deliver fresh reinforcements to your base.
rum01n_task_fortdefend: Defend the perimeter around the HQ
rum01n_task_trucksall: Don’t let a single truck escape
rum01n_task_narva: Take control of the city of Narva
rum01n_task_drg: Link up with the infiltrated special forces
rum01n_medal_1_c1: Finish the mission in any difficulty level
rum01n_medal_2_c1: Finish the mission in medium difficulty or higher
rum01n_medal_2_c2: Hold the crossroads and don't let any truck get away
rum01n_medal_3_c1: Finish the mission in hard difficulty
rum01n_medal_3_c2: Use the minimum amount of crews
rum01n_dial_0010: The city of Narva lies on the opposite bank of the Narva river, which marks the frontier with Estonia.
rum01n_dial_0020: We expect only limited resistance from reserve troops of the Estonian Defence League, and since the population is mostly Russian, there shouldn’t be much civilian resistance.
rum01n_dial_0030: But first we need to regroup our units. Who is in charge here?
rum01n_dial_0040: Lieutenant Petrov, I’m in charge here.
rum01n_dial_0050: Not anymore. Kommander, take control of these troops.
rum01n_dial_0060: Ha! Ha! You won’t be taking command of much here, we've got plenty of vehicles in storage, but no one to operate them.
rum01n_dial_0070: Well, ahem... Thanks, Lieutenant, we'll take it from here.
rum01n_dial_0080: Kommander, I will contact the nearby base and have them send crews for those vehicles.
rum01n_dial_0090: In the meantime, I suggest you take a look around and determine which vehicles the crews should be assigned to first.
rum01n_dial_0100: The first truck carrying vehicle crews has arrived. Send these crews to the unmanned armored vehicles you want to recover.
rum01n_dial_0110: Batalin reporting. My troops are in position. I’m ready to attack, just give the order!
rum01n_dial_0120: Hold on, Batalin. The Kommander is still working on recovering armored vehicles.
rum01n_dial_0130: Then hurry up Kommander! We must act quickly. Isn’t that right, Colonel?
rum01n_dial_0140: "Seizing the initiative and maintaining a high operational tempo keeps the enemy in a defensive mindset, making their actions predictable and easier to defeat."
rum01n_dial_0150: That’s from your memoirs, Comrade Colonel. I’ve memorized some passages by heart.
rum01n_dial_0160: BTR-82s — Those tin cans have less armor than a wheelbarrow, but they're fast and armed with a 30mm autocannon.
rum01n_dial_0170: Good old BMP-1s. Reliable as ever. Machines designed to fight in the middle of a nuclear apocalypse doesn’t fear a bit of rust and snow.
rum01n_dial_0180: BMP-2s — our backbone infantry fighting vehicles. Equipped with a 30mm autocannon and ATGMs.
rum01n_dial_0190: BMP-3s — now we're talking! This 100mm gun provides excellent fire support against infantry.
rum01n_dial_0200: MT-LBs — who would have thought I'de be retiring before they do?
rum01n_dial_0210: Akatsias — artillery conquers and infantry occupies. Ain't that right?
rum01n_dial_0220: T-72s — old, reliable, and smoking heavily. Ha! Just like me.
rum01n_dial_0230: That's a nice find, Kommander. T-80s were only issued to elite tank units. They've aged a bit now, of course, but who hasn't, huh?
rum01n_dial_0240: Look at that: an old Soviet Buratino! What the hell is it doing here? I’m not sure it’s the right tool for the job, I mean, we’re not here to raze the city to the ground.
rum01n_dial_0250: Tochka-U ballistic missile. Now that's old soviet tech! Still, as long as the missile manages to launch, it should come back down… eventually.
rum01n_dial_0260:
Oh, you found a Grad!
[Sing Katyusha] Выходила на берег Катюша.
Hehe!
rum01n_dial_0270: Strela-10 anti-aircraft vehicles — the Estonians don’t have any combat aircraft, but with American troops training nearby, you never know.
rum01n_dial_0280: A second truck transporting vehicle crews has arrived. Assign them to the vehicles you need the most Kommander.
rum01n_dial_0290: That should be enough to start the attack, Colonel. We don’t have all day.
rum01n_dial_0300: Absolutely not! We need at least twice as many vehicles, as well as support units, and...
rum01n_dial_0310: That wasn’t a question. We’re working on a very precise timeline, multiple operations like this are being launched simultaneously across all the Baltic States. If we don’t attack now, we’ll lose the element of surprise.
rum01n_dial_0320: Withholding information from me won’t get us anywhere! How am I supposed to make the right decisions if I don’t have all the cards in hand?
rum01n_dial_0330: Nobody has all the cards in hand, Colonel, not me, not you… especially not you! Otherwise, it would constitute a serious security risk for the entire operation.
rum01n_dial_0340: Alright, alright. Kommander, the bridge is right in front of us. This is a choke point, so we’d better cross it quickly and secure a bridgehead on the other side as fast as possible.
rum01n_dial_0350: The city might be mostly favorable to our presence, that doesn’t mean the garrison shares the same opinion.
rum01n_dial_0360: You need to capture the border guards’ headquarters as quickly as possible, and we might be able to take the city…
rum01n_dial_0390: I will continue to inform you when new vehicle crews become available, Kommander. Use them to reinforce your group or to replace any losses, if there are any.
rum01n_dial_0400: Many vehicles are in very poor condition. Our mechanics will try to get some of them back into working order over time. Sometimes it’s nothing, just a dead battery or a missing cable.
rum01n_dial_0410: New vehicle crews have arrived. Use them to reinforce our forces.
rum01n_dial_0420: A new truck full of vehicle crews has arrived; they’re waiting to be dispatched.
rum01n_dial_0430: Vehicle crews are waiting to be assigned, Kommander.
rum01n_dial_0440: The alarm has been sounded, we don't have much time.
rum01n_dial_0450: Head for the Estonian Defense League’s HQ before they can organize their defenses.
rum01n_dial_0460: Captain Batalin, advance on the left flank. Clear out that residential sector.
rum01n_dial_0470: Так точно,Tovarich Colonel! They won't understand what hit them!
rum01n_dial_0480: The HQ of the Estonian Defense League is ours. This should provide us with a short respite.
rum01n_dial_0490: I’m very fond of officers who act with caution, Kommander, but you’ll have to hurry this time. The enemy HQ is still not under our control, and we’ll lose the initiative if we don’t take it quickly.
rum01n_dial_0500: The Estonians are regrouping and seem to be preparing a counter-attack. Defend the perimeter of the fort. As long as we hold it, we keep control of the bridge.
rum01n_dial_0510: They've pushed you out of the fort, retake it immediately or this operation will fall apart!
rum01n_dial_0520: What kind of officer are you, Kommander?! A few Estonians reservists managed to push you back! You’re relieved of command.
rum01n_dial_0530:
CV90 armored vehicles spotted! They're from their elite Mechanized Scouts Battalion.
Something tells me this attack is not taking them completely by surprise.
rum01n_dial_0540: Looks like they've had enough! They're falling back and taking defensive positions in the buildings of the city center.
rum01n_dial_0550: Continue your progression through the city, Kommander. We need to clear all the pockets of resistance.
rum01n_dial_0560: The railway station is an important logistics hub. You can probably grab some supplies for your troops there.
rum01n_dial_0570: Capture this crossroad, Kommander. It controls the two main axes of the city. Nothing enters or leaves without us knowing if you control it.
rum01n_dial_0580: Comrade Colonel, they’re beginning to retreat on my flank as well. Their combat units are still holding the line, but I can see truck trucks trying to escape.
rum01n_dial_0590: They are heading your way, Kommander. You should be able to intercept them at the main crossroad if  you manage to hustle it a bit.
rum01n_dial_0600: Intercept those trucks  Kommander, don't give them a chance to fall back!
rum01n_dial_0610: Perfect, you hold the central crossroad. This is the beginning of the end for them.
rum01n_dial_0620: Comrade Colonel, the enemy is in full retreat on my side. I'm right on their heels, but they will arrive in force from your left flank Kommander. Be ready to greet them!
rum01n_dial_0630: Additional enemy units are converging in the direction fo the central crossroad. Don't let them retake it and intercept the retreating units, Kommander!
rum01n_dial_0640: Not a single truck intercepted?! How could you fail to destroy even a single one of them, Kommander?!
rum01n_dial_0650: Some trucks managed to get through, Kommander. Let’s just say you did your best... Too bad that wasn't enough!
rum01n_dial_0660: All the trucks have been destroyed. Don't you think I should get some credit for that? After all, I’m the one who made them flee in your direction!
rum01n_dial_0670: You've lost control of the crossroad, Kommander! Retake it immediately!
rum01n_dial_0680: Look at that! What a slaughter! Not a single retreating unit made it through.
rum01n_dial_0690: Some enemy units managed to get through, but they suffered heavy losses today.
rum01n_dial_0700: The remaining enemy forces are retreating from the town. This was a decisive victory today. I think it’s time for everyone to rest and refit.
rum01n_dial_0710: Negative, Colonel. Begin the pursuit immediately. Don't give them any respite.
rum01n_dial_0720: A group of partisans is willing to join our forces, Kommander. They're quite inexperienced and poorly equipped, so it's best to use them as rear-guard elements. Or cannon fodder... your choice.
rum01n_dial_0730: Kommander, we have a dormant cell infiltrated nearby. Link up with them, I'm sure they will prove to be very valuable assets.
rum01n_dial_0740: Look at that: Booker light tanks. The Americans are giving them away to anyone willing to take them these days.
rum01n_dial_0750: They’re not real tanks, just glorified assault guns! We’ll deal with them easily, Comrade Colonel!
rum01n_dial_0760: I don’t know, they sure look like tanks if you ask me.
rum02n_desc: Cut the retreat of the Estonian forces with a sea landing before they reach the capital city of Tallinn.
rum02n_task:
- Capture a foothold in the port
- Organize a naval landing
- Set up an ambush on the highway
rum02n_hint:
"My men never faltered in battle; the naval infantry bears a courage colder and harder than the Chechen winter itself."

Memoirs of captain Г. В. ДЕМИДОВ
rum02n_cine_010: So, what is the situation Colonel?
rum02n_cine_020: Narva is ours and half the Estonian army is on the run. They are heading towards Tallinn, their capital city, where the rest of their forces are concentrated.
rum02n_cine_030: We need to take this city as quickly as possible to force the Estonian government to surrender and bring this conflict to an end as soon as we can.
rum02n_cine_040: If we let their forces regroup, taking the city will prove a long and costful task.
rum02n_cine_050: Yes, actually I have a plan:
rum02n_cine_060: We have several Zubr Hovercrafts stationed in Saint Petersburg. They can transport heavy equipment including tanks and have the range and speed to reach Tallinn before the retreating troops.
rum02n_cine_070: Our landing zone will be Muuga, a large industrial harbor situated about 10 kilometers East of Tallinn.
rum02n_cine_080: There is a road from the harbor leading directly to highway E20 where we can intercept the retreating troops. The lake and swamps surrounding this crossroad create a bottleneck we can exploit to our advantage.
rum02n_brief_010: Captain Batalin, I hope you are not prone to seasickness?
rum02n_brief_020: Of course not, I am trained to fight in every possible situation.
rum02n_brief_030: That’s wonderful. The colonel was thinking of doing without your services for this mission and going with the Kommander alone.
rum02n_brief_050: I think he doubts about your abilities. But don’t worry I've convinced him to take you in.
rum02n_brief_060: I hope you'll rise to the occasion and show them what you're capable of.
rum02n_brief_070: I will not disappoint, thank you Lena.
rum02n_intel_01:
PDSS
Combat swimmers capable of moving on land as well as in water.
rum02n_intel_02:
Morskaya pekhota
The backbone of Russia’s coastal forces. A 7-man squad equipped with powerful RPG-27s. Versatile and suited for nearly any mission.
rum02n_intel_03:
Gornostrelki
Light infantry squad armed with SVD rifles and Metis light ATGMs. Effective against enemy infantry and light vehicles.
rum02n_intel_04:
Morskaya razvedka
A 5-man recon team capable of holding their own against both infantry and armored threats when needed.
rum02n_intel_05:
BMP-3F
A naval variant of the BMP-3 used by Russian Naval Infantry. Designed for amphibious landings and leading the coastal assault.
rum02n_intel_06:
T-80BV & T-80BVM
Two variants of the main battle tank used by Coastal troops. The BV is an affordable fire support platform. The BVM is a thoroughly modernized version with superior all-around performance.
rum02n_intel_07:
Ka-29
A versatile naval helicopter capable of transporting supplies or troops, and supporting allies with S-8 rockets.
rum02n_hint_01_text: PDSS are combat swimmers capable of moving on land as well as in water.
rum02n_hint_02_text: "Zubr" hovercrafts are available. Use them to deliver your armored vehicles to the coast. They can also support the assault with their multiple rocket launchers.
rum02n_hint_03_text: Ka-52K helicopters are now available. Armed with Ataka ATGMs, they are ideally suited for striking enemy vehicle columns.
rum02n_task_ambush: Set up an ambush on Highway E20
rum02n_task_crewrescue: Evacuate the crew to the hospital or the base: ({var,crew})
rum02n_medal_1_c1: Finish the mission in any difficulty level
rum02n_medal_1_c3: Complete the mission with no more than 12 units lost
rum02n_medal_2_c1: Finish the mission in medium difficulty or higher
rum02n_medal_2_c2: Capture your section of the port before Batalin takes his
rum02n_medal_2_c3: Complete the mission with no more than 7 units lost
rum02n_medal_3_c1: Finish the mission in hard difficulty
rum02n_medal_3_c2: Capture all the objectives in the port without Batalin's help
rum02n_medal_3_c3: Complete the mission with no more than 3 units lost
rum02n_dial_0010: The helicopter can't get any closer without risking detection. Drop the special forces in the water and and they'll swim the rest of the way.
rum02n_dial_0020: So, how cold is the water? Uhhuh... It's like jumping into an ice hole after a sauna. Perfect!
rum02n_dial_0030: Alright boys, you know what to do.
rum02n_dial_0040: According to our intelligence, the garrison here is minimal. But I don't want to take any chances nor give the enemy time to react.
rum02n_dial_0050: So the PDSS will eliminate the sentries and make sure the landing zone is clear
rum02n_dial_0060: Understood! Fast and clean, without unnecessary noise.
rum02n_dial_0070: Kommander, be careful. The PDSS are tough guys, but act in small teams, so don't underestimate the enemy.
rum02n_dial_0080: No sentries, no patrols. They must be afraid of the cold; all the harbor guards must be sitting in the warmth of their security posts.
rum02n_dial_0090: Perfect, find them, eliminate them, and send the signal once it's done.
rum02n_dial_0100: Why all these precautions Colonel? We could simply disembark tanks and BMPs on the shore and crush them!
rum02n_dial_0110: Captain, maybe one day you'll find yourself in an ambush and barely escape with your life like I did. That day you will understand the value of prudence.
rum02n_dial_0120: This story is not part of your memoirs...
rum02n_dial_0140: The guard post is under our control, great job guys!
rum02n_dial_0150: So? Can we start the landing now?
rum02n_dial_0160: The shoreline has been secured, we can send the first hovercrafts. Kommander your targets are these 4 key locations on the right flank.
rum02n_dial_0170: Capitain Batalin, you will handle the positions on the left flank.
rum02n_dial_0180: The fleet allocated us several Zubr hovercrafts. Powerful machines, from a glorious time... We only have a handful left, so use them wisely.
rum02n_dial_0190: Comrade Colonel, is that all? Sounds like too easy a task for me. I bet I will be done with my zones before the Kommander captures one of his.
rum02n_dial_0200: This is not a competition, Batalin! This is a combat operation! Help me secure the perimeter if you're so afraid of being idle.
rum02n_dial_0210: Yes sir! I'll give a warm, high-explosive welcome to any enemy unit approaching the harbor!
rum02n_dial_0220: What's your favorite instrument Kommander? Mine is Stalin's organ! Огонь!
rum02n_dial_0230: Get away from here Kommander! This is my area of ​​operation!
rum02n_dial_0240: Kommander, this is my part of the harbor! Mind your own tasks!
rum02n_dial_0250: Здравствуйте, Colonel. How is the operation going? Have you started the landing yet?
rum02n_dial_0260: We're in the middle of it, so if you don't mind I would like to focus on the task at hand. You'll get my full report after the operation.
rum02n_dial_0270: Understood, I'll be waiting for it.
rum02n_dial_0280: Nothing has changed, we thought the burden of bureaucracy was a thing of the past, but the only thing that has changed is the color of the uniform.
rum02n_dial_0290: From my point of view, bureaucracy has never looked so good!
rum02n_dial_0300: Batalin, you should think with your upper head and focus on your objectives!
rum02n_dial_0310: So, where do you stand, Captain? The Kommander has already completed his assignments. I thought you were supposed to be the fastest!
rum02n_dial_0320: But I encountered more resistance and helped secure the perimeter!
rum02n_dial_0330: Nobody forced you, Captain. So don't be so pretentious next time, and don't bite off more than you can chew!
rum02n_dial_0340: Well, Captain, I see the Kommander ended up assisting you after all.
rum02n_dial_0350: But I never asked for help!
rum02n_dial_0360: Are you having trouble reaching your objectives, Kommander? Mine were completed a long time ago.
